translation: And being in agony, he prayed more earnestly; and his sweat became like clots of blood, falling down upon the earth.

alternate_translations.translation: And in his anguish he prayed all the more intensely, and his sweat was as though it were drops of blood, running down to the ground.
alternate_translations.notes: “ἐν ἀγωνίᾳ” can mean “anguish/agony,” and “ὡσεὶ θρόμβοι αἵματος” literally refers to “clots of blood,” which some translators render more loosely as “drops of blood,” capturing the sense without insisting on “clots.”

alternate_translations.translation: And he was in agony and prayed more intensely; and his sweat appeared like drops of blood, trickling down to the ground.
alternate_translations.notes: “προσηύχετο ἐκτενέστερον” is best rendered as “prayed more earnestly/intensely.” “καταβαίνοντες ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν” can be rendered as “trickling/falling down to the ground,” preserving the image of downward flow.

notes: This passage describes intense suffering and the phenomenon sometimes called “hematidrosis” (sweat resembling blood). The phrase “ὡσεὶ θρόμβοι αἵματος” is vivid: literally “like clots of blood,” though some English versions choose “drops of blood” to convey the outward appearance. The overall meaning is that his distress was severe and his sweat took on a bloodlike appearance, descending to the ground.
